steps.title: Spot the contrasting claim about temperate female birdsong
steps.content: Text 2 states that female birdsong is more common among temperate species than currently assumed, while Text 1 treats a singing Cassin’s finch as evidence of a male, implying females rarely sing in temperate zones.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B says the authors would disagree about assuming temperate‑species females don’t sing, which directly reflects Text 2’s claim of more common female song versus Text 1’s male‑only assumption.
The other three either misplace the comparison to tropical species, reverse the relationship, or introduce claims not made by either author.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
